questions.question: Which statement best explains why unsaturated carbon compounds are generally more reactive than saturated carbon compounds?
questions.option_1: Unsaturated compounds have fewer hydrogen atoms attached to carbon
questions.option_2: Unsaturated compounds contain only single bonds between carbon atoms
questions.option_3: Unsaturated compounds have double or triple bonds between carbon atoms
questions.option_4: Unsaturated compounds form only straight carbon chains

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes a heterogeneous mixture?
questions.option_1: Its composition is not uniform throughout the mixture
questions.option_2: Its components cannot be separated by physical methods
questions.option_3: Its composition remains fixed and constant always
questions.option_4: Its particles are uniformly distributed at particle level

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes an ion?
questions.option_1: A molecule containing similar atoms only
questions.option_2: A compound formed by covalent bonds
questions.option_3: A charged atom or group of atoms
questions.option_4: A neutral atom formed by chemical bonding

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes the atomic number of an element?
questions.option_1: Total mass of all subatomic particles
questions.option_2: Total number of nucleons in the atom
questions.option_3: Number of protons present in the nucleus
questions.option_4: Number of neutrons present in the nucleus

questions.question: Which statement is correct regarding the definition of biodegradable substances?
questions.option_1: Substances that are broken by physical process
questions.option_2: Substances that are broken by chemical process
questions.option_3: Substances that are broken by biological process
questions.option_4: Substances that are broken by mechanical process

questions.question: Which substance has a molecular mass of 44 u?
Why are brackets used in the formula Ca(OH)₂?
Why are metals such as sodium and calcium extracted by electrolytic reduction instead of heating with carbon?
questions.option_1: They have greater affinity for oxygen than carbon
questions.option_2: They decompose when heated with carbon
questions.option_3: They form unstable oxides at high temperature
questions.option_4: They exist only as molten chlorides in nature

questions.question: Why are non-biodegradable substances harmful for the environment?
questions.option_2: Needs heat and temperature for breakdown, releases harmful chemicals
questions.option_3: Can be broken down by bacteria

questions.question: Why are sulphide ores usually converted into oxides before the extraction of metals?
questions.option_1: Sulphide ores contain more gangue materials
questions.option_2: Metal oxides are easier to reduce than sulphides
questions.option_3: Metal sulphides react faster with carbon
questions.option_4: Sulphide ores are unstable at room temperature

questions.question: Why is alcohol considered a cleaner fuel than petrol?
Why is mercury obtained from cinnabar ore through two heating steps instead of direct reduction?
questions.option_1: Mercury sulphide must first change into oxide
questions.option_3: Mercury metal reacts with oxygen on heating
questions.option_4: Mercury oxide forms only in absence of air

questions.question: Why is nitrogen gas used to prevent oxidation in bags containing foods such as chips?
questions.option_1: Nitrogen gas inhibits oxidation by replacing oxygen.
questions.option_2: Nitrogen gas enhances the taste of the food.
